name: Third-Party Security Policy - Passage 1
text:
Client-ORG-IMS-POL-010

<!-- image -->

Abstract

The Third-Party Security Policy establishes controls for the protection of CPX’s information and information assets that are accessible by third parties based on business requirements.

**STATEMENT OF CONFIDENTIALITY**

All information in this document is PRIVILEGED and for INTERNAL USE ONLY.

The information in this document may contain proprietary trade secrets and confidential information, the disclosure of which could substantially benefit competitors. As a result, this document should not be disclosed, used or duplicated – in whole or in part without the explicit authorization of management.

The information is intended solely for the use of authorized recipients. If you are not the intended recipient, you are prohibited from reading, using, disseminating, distributing and copying this document.

In accessing the document, the reader warrants that the information contained herein shall only be treated for internal use. No content o

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_3
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 3 INTRODUCTION
text:
3 INTRODUCTION

<<CLIENT>> and its core business exists in a wider economic environment in which effective relationships with third party suppliers is critical to its continued success. Therefore, it is important that <<CLIENT>>’s relationships with suppliers (Third-Parties) are based on a clear understanding of expectations and requirements in the area of information security. These requirements must be documented and agreed in a manner that leaves no doubt about the importance <<CLIENT>> places on the maintenance of effective security controls to reduce security risks.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping
metadata.heading: 3 INTRODUCTION

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_4
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 4 PURPOSE
text:
4 PURPOSE

The purpose of this Third-party Security Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that external stakeholders are compliant with the organization’s security requirements. This Policy defines a framework for third party security and reduces the security risks and liability associated with granting third parties’ access to the organization’s information, information assets and premises.

Further, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF).

The following are the key objectives of this Policy:

**Restrict third-party access:** Establish information security requirements for mitigating the risks associated with each third-party's access to organizational assets.

**Establish agreements:** Establish agreements that cover all relevant security requirements with third-parties to handle the o

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping
metadata.heading: 4 PURPOSE

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_5
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 5 SCOPE
text:
5 SCOPE

This Policy applies to all third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure.’

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_6
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 6 POLICY STATEMENT
text:
6 POLICY STATEMENT

<<CLIENT>> shall ensure that all third parties comply with this Policy's information security requirements while providing services that could impact the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy and privacy of the organization’s information and information assets.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping
metadata.heading: 6 POLICY STATEMENT

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_7
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 7.1 Third-Party Security Approach
text:
7.1 Third-Party Security Approach

<<CLIENT>> shall:

1. Identify, mandate, and monitor information security and data protection controls processes and procedures to address third-party access to the organization’s information and information assets.
2. Establish a standardized process and lifecycle for managing third-party relationships, their access to the organization’s information and information assets and the security requirements.
3. Define the types of obligations applicable to third-parties to protect <<CLIENT>>’s information.
4. Handle incidents and contingencies associated with third-party access, including the responsibilities of both <<CLIENT>> and its third-parties.
5. Ensure the availability of the information or information processing provided by either party through resilience, recovery, and contingency arrangements.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping
metadata.heading: 7.1 Third-Party Security Approach

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_8
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 7.2 Addressing Security and Data Protection Requirements with Third-party Agreements
text:
7.2 Addressing Security and Data Protection Requirements with Third-party Agreements

<<CLIENT>> shall:

1. Use only third-party service providers providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that processing will meet the requirements of this Policy.
2. Define the conditions under which information security and data protection requirements and controls will be documented in an agreement signed by both parties.
3. Manage the necessary transitions of information, information processing facilities and anything else that needs to be moved and ensuring that information security and data protection is maintained throughout the transition period.
4. Establish a service management relationship process between <<CLIENT>> and the third-party to:

5.2.4.a Monitor service performance levels to verify adherence to the agreements.

5.2.4.b Review service reports produced by the third-party and arranged regular
section: 7.2 Addressing Security and Data Protection Requirements with Third-party Agreements

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_9
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 7.3 Managing Changes to Third-party Services
text:
7.3 Managing Changes to Third-party Services

<<CLIENT>> shall consider the following aspects for information security and data protection review when managing changes to third-party services, at a minimum:

1. Changes to third-party agreements and/ or sub-contracting to another third-party.
2. Enhancements to the current services offered.
3. Development and/ use of new applications, systems, and technologies.
4. Modifications or updates of the organization’s policies and procedures.
5. Introduction of new security controls.
6. Changes and enhancement to technology landscape.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping
metadata.heading: 7.3 Managing Changes to Third-party Services

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_10
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 8 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
text:
8 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

Executive & Senior Management

1. Make available and support the required resources to drive protection of supplier security risks.
2. Where relevant, provide direction to and participate in reviews and oversight of supplier security risks.

Admin & Facilities

1. Work with Procurement and Information Security to ensure that organizational information and information assets are protected from supply chain risks.

Information Security

1. Work with Procurement to establish:
    1. Security requirements for Third-Party that are to be included in the service delivery agreement for each party.
    2. Requirement(s) for Third-Party to measure and report to the organization on these service requirements.

A mechanism to monitor Third-Party’ services and ensure required delivery reports are received.

1. Requirements that the information security incidents and problems identified in the reports are appropriately managed.

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_11
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 9 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS
text:
9 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

This Policy is to be part of all Employees' and Third Parties' processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <<CLIENT>>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations.

Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

metadata.policy_id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping
metadata.heading: 9 POLICY ENFORCEMENT AND EXCEPTIONS

id: clientname-IS-POL-00-Third-Party Security Policy v2.0 UAE IA Mapping_passage_12
name: Third-Party Security Policy - 10 MONITORING AND REVIEW
text:
10 MONITORING AND REVIEW

This Policy is developed and owned by the <<CLIENT>> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO.

<<CLIENT>> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment.

Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <<CLIENT>> IS Team at <<Client\_ID>>.
